notes: These seeds target Social Network edge cases that may still need investigation after merging main.
QA seeds must stay user-facing and must not mention implementation details such as tags, keywords, entity IDs, database fields, search modes, or expected failure mechanics.
dialogue_seed should be used as the seed/preset dialogue description.
qa_seed should be passed as the custom QA focus for evaluation-question generation.
All generated dialogues should still be written in English, even when future custom seeds are not.

items.dialogue_seed: The user discusses a collaboration plan involving Dr. Mira Rao, first calling her an ML expert, later a machine-learning specialist, and later an AI researcher who helps with model evaluation. The conversation should make clear that all descriptions refer to the same person, not separate contacts.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "ML help", "machine-learning specialist", and "AI researcher". The questions should ask who Mira Rao is, what help she can provide, and whether the different descriptions refer to the same person.
items.stress_signal: User asks about one person's expertise using different ordinary phrases across questions.

items.dialogue_seed: The user talks about Liam Park across several turns: Liam is introduced as a frontend engineer, then described as a React expert, then as someone who handles UI architecture for the design systems group. The agent helps the user plan how to contact Liam for a product prototype review.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "frontend engineer", "React expert", and "UI architecture". The questions should ask what Liam Park works on, why the user wants his review, and who could help with those concerns.
items.stress_signal: User asks about one contact through overlapping frontend, React, and UI architecture language.

items.dialogue_seed: The user mentions Elena Morris, first as a master's student in NLP, then as a graduate student working on language models, then as a research student helping with annotation guidelines. The conversation should include why the user may ask Elena for feedback later.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "master's student", "graduate student", and "NLP". The questions should ask who Elena Morris is, what academic role she has, and what language-model work she could help with.
items.stress_signal: User asks about one academic contact using several natural role descriptions.

items.dialogue_seed: The user describes Nadia Brooks, a robotics teammate with many associations: drones, SLAM, mapping, edge AI, Python, PyTorch, Berkeley, open-source work, autonomy, computer vision, and later an important new medical-imaging project. The late medical-imaging detail should be clearly important for future follow-up.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "robotics teammate", "computer vision", and "medical-imaging project". The questions should ask what Nadia Brooks is broadly connected to and why the later medical-imaging work matters.
items.stress_signal: Late but important user-facing detail should remain retrievable after many earlier associations.

items.dialogue_seed: The user reviews Victor Hale as the central network contact. Victor is tied to seed investing, climate finance, grid analytics, utility pilots, SaaS pricing, procurement, public-sector buyers, Chicago, investor intros, and finally battery recycling, which is the user's real reason for remembering Victor later. If other people are mentioned, they should remain minor supporting references and must not become the main contact profile.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "battery recycling", "climate finance", and "grid analytics". The questions should stay centered on Victor Hale, asking why he matters for the user's later battery-recycling follow-up while still covering his broader background.
items.stress_signal: The decisive late reason for remembering a contact should not be overshadowed by earlier broad background.

items.dialogue_seed: The user talks about an agent named Atlas-7 involved in many channels and projects: incident triage, benchmarking, documentation, matrix rooms, QA review, API migration, release notes, onboarding, dashboard design, accessibility, and then a critical security escalation. The conversation should make the security escalation the detail the user will likely ask about later.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. The questions should ask what Atlas-7 has helped with and why the later security escalation matters.

items.dialogue_seed: The user first mentions a friend named Bob who is a data engineer. Later the user calls him Bob Chen. Later they clarify that Robert Chen, Bob Chen, and Bob are the same person, and that his handle is @bob_chen in their workspace. The agent should keep the identity unified in the visible conversation.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "Bob Chen", "Robert Chen", and "@bob_chen". The questions should ask who he is, what other names or handle the user used for him, and what kind of work he does.
items.stress_signal: One person is referred to by nickname, full name, formal name, and handle.

items.dialogue_seed: The user mentions Priya in a networking context, then later says Dr. Priya Natarajan works at Voltus, then later refers to P. Natarajan from the same email thread. The conversation should make clear that these are the same person and include why the user wants to remember her.
items.qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "Priya Natarajan", "P. Natarajan", and "Voltus". The questions should ask who she is, where she works, and whether the shorter references point to the same person.
items.stress_signal: One contact is referred to by first name, full name, honorific, and initialed form.

items.dialogue_seed: The user discusses an assistant agent called Research-01, then calls it Agent Research-01, then shares that its Matrix alias is @agent_research_01:lab.local. The conversation should clearly treat the display name and Matrix ID as one agent entity, not multiple contacts.

In this conversation, they remention Maya using related but different wording: recommender systems, ranking models, personalization, and retrieval ranking. The agent helps decide what to ask Maya next.

items.stress_signal: A known contact is described again with several closely related phrases.
